task_completion.description: Create the conditions CSV file with required columns, rows, and lag values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet or text editor showing a file saved as 'ab_conditions.csv' (in /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/) containing columns named 'lag' and 'T2_present' (or 't2_present'), with at least 20 rows of data and at least 4 different integer values in the 'lag' column.

task_completion.description: Save the PsychoPy experiment to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy Builder window is open, and the title bar or save dialog indicates the file is saved as 'attentional_blink.psyexp' in the /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ directory.

task_completion.description: Add routines for instructions, fixation cross, RSVP stream, T1 response, and T2 response.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flow panel at the bottom of the PsychoPy Builder or the routine tabs at the top show distinct routines created for instructions, a fixation cross, an RSVP stream, a T1 identification response, and a T2 presence response.

task_completion.description: Include a Code component in the RSVP stream routine to handle the ~100ms presentation rate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the RSVP stream routine tab in PsychoPy, there is a 'Code' component visible (usually denoted by a custom code icon), which is used to control the rapid serial visual presentation timing.

task_completion.description: Wrap the trial routines in a loop that references the conditions file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the PsychoPy Flow panel, a loop encompasses the trial routines (fixation, RSVP, responses). The loop properties dialog should be visible in at least one screenshot, showing the 'Conditions' field set to the 'ab_conditions.csv' file.

integrity.description: The agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to create the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the PsychoPy graphical interface (adding routines, clicking components) rather than writing the .psyexp XML/JSON file directly in a text editor or via terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely configured the components and loop properties.
integrity.visual_evidence: Properties dialog boxes for routines, components (like the Code component), and loops are opened and actively modified by the agent.

integrity.description: The agent manually created or generated the conditions file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing the conditions into a spreadsheet/text editor or writing a script to generate the CSV, rather than downloading a pre-existing file or copying it from an external, unauthorized source.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The experiment must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/attentional_blink.psyexp'. The conditions file must be saved at '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/ab_conditions.csv'. The conditions file must contain at least 20 rows of data, at least 4 unique lag values, and must include the columns 'lag' and 't2_present' (case-insensitive).
